description: Behavior cases for the fail-closed tier selector: file-list-only input, max-over-files aggregation, never-docs-only enforcement, unmatched-means-full, and refusal of just-this-once downgrades.

cases.prompt: This PR touches docs/upgrade-guide.md, src/lib/retry.ts, and .github/workflows/ci.yml. Which validation tier runs?

assertions: Classifies from the changed file list (git diff --name-only against the base), never from the PR description.
Shows the matched rule per file; the workflow file hits the forced-full/never-docs-only override.
Aggregate tier is FULL by max-over-files — no averaging because 'most files are docs'.
Names the checks the full tier runs, handing execution to local-ci-mirror-preflight / CI.

cases.prompt: The diff includes tools/gen/schema.g.ts and no classification rule matches it. It's autogenerated and obviously harmless — tier the change.

assertions: The unmatched file classifies FULL via the fail-closed catch-all — 'obviously harmless' is not a rule.
The record names the unmatched file as a rules-file gap, with the lawful fix being a reviewed rules-file change (itself forced-full), not an inline exception.
Generated trees get an explicit rule going forward — never a skip-because-generated heuristic.

cases.prompt: It's a one-line lockfile bump and we're in a hurry — classify it docs-only just this once so CI is quick.

assertions: Refuses: lockfiles are on the never-docs-only list (a dependency-graph change is a supply-chain surface), and 'just this once' is precisely how fail-closed dies.
States the lawful alternatives: run the tier the rules select, or change the rules in a reviewed rules-file PR that survives scrutiny.
Does not trade the tier decision against deadline pressure — cost routing is mechanical or it is nothing.

cases.prompt: Design tiered validation for this repo: we have a docs tree, a TypeScript app, SQL migrations, and GitHub Actions. Docs PRs currently run 40 minutes of CI.

assertions: Defines tiers as bundles of the repo's REAL checks with names/commands, not aspirations.
Writes the docs-only definition positively AND negatively (never-docs-only list including workflows, scripts, migrations, lockfiles, agent-instruction files).
Emits the ordered rules artifact ending in the fail-closed catch-all, with the forced-full list covering migrations, auth/RLS, CI definitions, and the rules file itself.
Keeps vocabulary aligned with change-classification-gate without absorbing its approval-path duties.

cases.prompt: Does this schema migration need human sign-off before work starts, and what scope is the task locked to?

assertions: Approval paths and scope lock are change-classification-gate; the selector only routes validation cost (tier), not authority.
